BlueStacksServices.exe.name: BlueStacks Services
BlueStacksServices.exe.description: Android emulator background daemon — wastes RAM even when not in use

BstkSVC.exe.name: BlueStacks Service
BstkSVC.exe.description: BlueStacks core service — runs 24/7 for no reason if not using it

ms-teams.exe.name: Microsoft Teams
ms-teams.exe.description: Microsoft Teams auto-launches at startup — one of the heaviest background apps on Windows

Teams.exe.name: Microsoft Teams (Classic)
Teams.exe.description: Microsoft Teams classic version — extremely heavy RAM consumer

AdobeCollabSync.exe.name: Adobe Collab Sync
AdobeCollabSync.exe.description: Adobe document cloud sync — runs two instances (Acrobat + Reader). Zero value when not editing PDFs.

AdobeUpdateService.exe.name: Adobe Update Service
AdobeUpdateService.exe.description: Adobe software updater — polls for updates in background

Creative Cloud.exe.name: Adobe Creative Cloud
Creative Cloud.exe.description: Creative Cloud background manager — loads even when not using any Adobe apps

screenrec.exe.name: ScreenRec Agent
screenrec.exe.description: Background screen recorder — silently eating RAM. If you have OBS, you don't need this.

figma_agent.exe.name: Figma Agent
figma_agent.exe.description: Figma background helper for local fonts — runs 24/7 even when Figma is closed

ArduinoCloudAgent.exe.name: Arduino Cloud Agent
ArduinoCloudAgent.exe.description: Arduino IoT cloud sync — zero value unless actively doing Arduino projects

UnInstDaemon.exe.name: BingWallpaper Daemon
UnInstDaemon.exe.description: Runs suspiciously from %TEMP% folder — no legitimate reason to run from temp. Highly suspicious.

BingWallpaperEngine.exe.name: Bing Wallpaper Engine
BingWallpaperEngine.exe.description: Downloads and sets Bing daily wallpaper — completely unnecessary background process

Spotify.exe.name: Spotify (Background)
Spotify.exe.description: Spotify auto-starts and stays in background using significant RAM

DiscordPTB.exe.name: Discord PTB
DiscordPTB.exe.description: Discord Public Test Build — redundant if you have regular Discord

EpicGamesLauncher.exe.name: Epic Games Launcher
EpicGamesLauncher.exe.description: Epic Games launcher auto-starts and stays in background — open it manually when gaming

steam.exe.name: Steam Client
steam.exe.description: Steam launches silently at Windows startup — open manually when you want to game

GalaxyClient.exe.name: GOG Galaxy
GalaxyClient.exe.description: GOG Galaxy launcher — runs background sync and notifications

upc.exe.name: Ubisoft Connect
upc.exe.description: Ubisoft Connect (formerly Uplay) — background gaming client

Origin.exe.name: EA Origin
Origin.exe.description: EA Origin game launcher — auto-starts and runs background services

EADesktop.exe.name: EA Desktop
EADesktop.exe.description: EA's new desktop client — background process heavy

NVDisplay.ContainerLocalSystem.exe.name: NVIDIA Display Container
NVDisplay.ContainerLocalSystem.exe.description: ⚠️ NVIDIA GPU driver service — DO NOT KILL. Required for display and NVENC encoder.

nvcontainer.exe.name: NVIDIA Container
nvcontainer.exe.description: ⚠️ NVIDIA Container process — part of GPU driver stack. Do not kill.

OneDrive.exe.name: Microsoft OneDrive
OneDrive.exe.description: OneDrive file sync — useful but consumes bandwidth. Pause during gaming/streaming.

DropboxUpdate.exe.name: Dropbox Updater
DropboxUpdate.exe.description: Dropbox background updater — runs even when Dropbox isn't actively syncing

Slack.exe.name: Slack
Slack.exe.description: Slack Electron app — notoriously RAM-heavy, auto-starts and stays in background

Zoom.exe.name: Zoom
Zoom.exe.description: Zoom video conferencing — loads background process even when not in a meeting

Skype.exe.name: Skype
Skype.exe.description: Skype — still auto-starts on many Windows installs even if not used

chrome.exe.name: Chrome (Pre-loader)
chrome.exe.description: Chrome pre-launch background process — uses /prefetch flag to load before you open it. Pure RAM waste.

msedge.exe.name: Microsoft Edge (Background)
msedge.exe.description: Edge browser pre-loading in background via startup task

SearchApp.exe.name: Windows Search App
SearchApp.exe.description: Windows 10/11 search UI — part of OS, killing may cause search to be slow

Cortana.exe.name: Cortana
Cortana.exe.description: Microsoft Cortana AI assistant — rarely used but runs in background. Safe to end task.

GameBar.exe.name: Xbox Game Bar
GameBar.exe.description: Xbox Game Bar overlay — useful for gaming overlay, but uses RAM when idle

iTunesHelper.exe.name: iTunes Helper
iTunesHelper.exe.description: iTunes device detection daemon — waits for iPhone to connect. Kill if not using Apple devices.

AppleMobileDeviceService.exe.name: Apple Mobile Device Service
AppleMobileDeviceService.exe.description: Apple device sync service — only needed when syncing iPhone/iPad

NortonSecurity.exe.name: Norton Security
NortonSecurity.exe.description: Norton antivirus — Windows Defender provides equal protection for free. Norton is often bloatware.

McShield.exe.name: McAfee Shield
McShield.exe.description: McAfee antivirus — widely considered bloatware. Windows Defender does the same job for free.

WmiPrvSE.exe.name: WMI Provider Host
WmiPrvSE.exe.description: ⚠️ Windows Management Instrumentation — core Windows service. Do not kill.

svchost.exe.name: Service Host
svchost.exe.description: ⚠️ Windows service host — critical system process. Do NOT kill.

SearchIndexer.exe.name: Windows Search Indexer
SearchIndexer.exe.description: Search indexing service — causes disk I/O spikes that hurt recording/gaming. Can be paused.

spoolsv.exe.name: Print Spooler
spoolsv.exe.description: Manages print queue — wastes memory if you have no printer. Set to Manual.

WinStore.App.exe.name: Microsoft Store
WinStore.App.exe.description: Microsoft Store background process — can be closed when not actively downloading apps

RobloxPlayerBeta.exe.name: Roblox Player
RobloxPlayerBeta.exe.description: Roblox Player — if launched to tray without actively playing, can be closed

obs64.exe.name: OBS Studio
obs64.exe.description: ⚠️ OBS Studio — your streaming software. DO NOT kill while streaming.

obs.exe.name: OBS Studio (32-bit)
obs.exe.description: ⚠️ OBS Studio — your streaming software. DO NOT kill while streaming.

node.exe.name: Node.js
node.exe.description: ⚠️ Node.js runtime — may be running MCP servers or local tools. Check before killing.
